content: The distance between the foci of a hyperbola is 16 and its eccentricity is $$\sqrt{2}$$. Its equation is
explanation:
Given, distance between the foci $$=16$$ and

$$\begin{aligned} e & =\sqrt{2} \\ 2 a e & =16 \\ a e & =8 \\ \Rightarrow \quad a & =\frac{8}{\sqrt{2}}=4 \sqrt{2} \end{aligned}$$

So,

$$\begin{aligned} & b^2=a^2\left(e^2-1\right) \\ & b^2=32(2-1)=32 \end{aligned}$$

Equation of hyperbola is

$$\begin{aligned} & \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \\ & \frac{x^2}{32}-\frac{y^2}{32}=1 \\ &\Rightarrow \quad x^2-y^2 =32 \end{aligned}$$

content: The distance between the foci of a hyperbola is 16 and its eccentricity is $\sqrt{2}$. Its equation is
explanation:
Given, distance between the foci of hyperbola is 16 .

    $$ \therefore \quad 2 a e=16 $$

    and eccentricity $(e)=\sqrt{2}$

    From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

    $$ a=4 \sqrt{2} $$

    We know that in hyperbola,

    $$ \begin{array}{ll} & (a e)^2=a^2+b^2 \\ \Rightarrow & (8)^2=32+b^2 \\ \Rightarrow & b^2=32 \end{array} $$

    $$ \begin{aligned} & \therefore \text { Equation of hyperbola is } \\ & \qquad \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{x^2}{32}-\frac{y^2}{32}=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x^2-y^2=32 \end{aligned} $$
